mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market triggers recession fears and forces aggressive Federal Reserve policy pivots, causing immediate equity sell-offs and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4 percentage points within a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between industrial production growth and housing starts signals a supply-demand mismatch that compresses corporate profit margins, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO growth minus HOUST growth) shows no statistically significant predictive power for 20-day returns (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression controlling for market beta.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation during a rate-hiking cycle forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy longer than markets price in, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or neutral when CPIAUCSL accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, invalidating the inflation-restriction transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Rising private sector credit demand reflects improving business investment and consumer confidence, directly supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month Spearman correlation between RSAFS month-over-month changes and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.05, indicating no persistent lead-lag relationship.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial output signals an impending economic slowdown that historically precedes Federal Reserve rate cuts, triggering a relief rally and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 105.0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX generates negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances following the threshold breach, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 200 basis points over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong employment growth and declining unemployment rates confirm a robust economic expansion that sustains corporate earnings momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative directional accuracy below 45% over a 3-year walk-forward test, or the interaction coefficient in a logistic regression model is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating real GDP growth during a late-cycle expansion triggers profit margin compression and valuation de-rating, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive when GDPC1 quarterly growth decelerates by more than 0.5 percentage points from the prior quarter, breaking the late-cycle earnings contraction premise.
